learning_outcomes.description: demonstrate an understanding of early modern drama and specific knowledge of a range of plays that are representative of the early modern period;
interpret and analyse the historical, cultural, literary, and dramatic contexts of early modern plays in critical and creative ways
locate relevant secondary materials for scholarly research on early modern plays, and apply relevant methods and research techniques; and

write and communicate accurately and clearly in relation to early modern drama; apply relevant critical vocabulary and appropriate theoretical terminology; and develop arguments supported by relevant evidence in your research and analysis.

assessments.task_description:
This assessment involves demonstrating your ability to manage and take responsibility for your own learning. It comprises:

·        Engagement (20%) (2 marks x 10 tutorials): This requires preparation (completing the required readings and worksheets); active participation in discussions and activities; and completing in-class writing exercises. For the worksheets you need to print out a hard copy, handwrite your answers (unless you have a SAP in place that allows typewritten answers) and bring the worksheet to class. Your worksheet will be checked by your tutor, signed and returned to you the following tute.

·        Peer Review Workshop of Draft Abstracts and Annotated Bibliographies (Week 11) (10%): This requires bringing 3 hard copies of your Draft Abstract and Annotated Bibliography for your research project to the workshop, and providing constructive feedback to others in your cohort on their drafts.

·        Management of Learning Summary: submit 30/10/25 (week 13) Fill in the Management of Learning Summary (see template on BB) with the scans/photos of each of your 10 completed worksheets signed by your tutor. Also fill in the table of attended tutorials. Submit via the link on the course site.

Criteria:

·        Evidence of a reasonable attempt at each of the components of the Management of Learning.

Submission: for submission of the Management of Learning Summary, upload a file to the Turnitin link via the course BB site. The link for submission will become available in the Assessment folder the day before the due date.

Extension/Alternative assessment option:

·       If you miss one or more tutorials without a documented reason consistent with UQ's extension policy, you can make up the marks for one missed tutorial at the alternative session held in the lecture slot of week 13. If you miss more than one tutorial with documented reasons consistent with UQ's extension policy, you must contact the course coordinator within one week of your return to studies in order to make alternative arrangements

·        It is not possible to make up the marks for the Peer Review Workshop.

AI Policy

Secure Assessment: This assessment is designed to protect academic integrity and assess your understanding of course concepts. You must only use the approved materials and tools for this assessment task. Use of prohibited materials in this task may be investigated and treated as academic misconduct. See assessment instructions for more details.

           Engagement: Note that for in-class writing exercises (generally 10-15 mins), the task will be handwritten and without the aid of devices (except if a student has a SAP in place for any medical condition affecting their ability to handwrite). This assessment task is to be completed in-person.

Library Materials and Gen-AI: Students are also reminded that library materials may not be entered into AI technologies without express permission: Policies and guidelines - Library - The University of Queensland

“You may not share, re-publish, copy or enter into AI technologies any part of a resource accessed from our online collections unless expressly permitted in the licence terms for that resource”

“Except as permitted under each Publisher's terms and conditions of use, you must not:

Use UQ Library resource content as an input to generative AI products or services”


For referencing AI/MT see the Library guide: https://guides.library.uq.edu.au/referencing/ai-tools-assignments

For referencing AI in MLA style see: How do I cite generative AI in MLA style? from the official MLA style website.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission: for submission of the Management of Learning Summary, upload a file to the Turnitin link via the course BB site. The link for submission will become available in the Assessment folder the day before the due date.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.category: Creative Production/ Exhibition, Essay/ Critique, Product/ Design, Reflection
assessments.task_description:
Word Count: 1000 words maximum

Instructions:

·        Part 1: Design a creative response to one of the plays on the course. This can be in the form of a:

o  Zine (10 pages max); or

o  Creative journal (10 pages max); or

o  Rewrite of a scene (750 words max) from a play.

Design elements for the zine or creative journal option could include collage, drawn or traced images, and cut and pasted quotations from the play. The creative journal could, for example, reflect on your reading/watching experiences of the play, or be an imagined journal by one of the characters in the play. The rewrite of a scene could imagine the scene from an alternative perspective or from a particular character’s point of view, or adapt the scene to a modern setting or different context. You are encouraged to use your imagination and creative skills in this exercise. At the same time, the response should evidence your knowledge of the play.

·        Part 2: Reflection on process (300 words): Reflect on your process and experience in designing your creative response. For example, did your creative response lead to some new insights on the play for you? What meanings might a reader/viewer find in your work?

Criteria:

·        Creativity (10 marks)

·        Reflection on process (10 marks)

·        Evidence of knowledge of the focus play (5 marks)

Submission: Fill in the Creative Response template on BB which will include your reflection on process and photos/scans of the zine/creative journal (if chosen) or scene rewrite. Upload a file to the Turnitin link via the course BB site. The link for submission will become available in the folder the day before the due date.

Exhibition: Students will have the opportunity, if they wish, to have their work for the Creative Response included in an exhibition at the Fryer library at the end of semester (details to be confirmed).

AI Policy

Open Assessment: In this assessment you can use resources such as your notes, course materials, and other tools, including AI, while completing the task. The focus is not on memorising information, but on understanding, applying judgement, and demonstrating your own thinking.

Note that:

·        while this assessment is classified as open assessment, so the use of AI is permissible, your work is more likely to evidence genuine creativity and reflection if the task is not outsourced to AI;

·        the use of AI may result in errors and fabricated information and sources eg invented quotations. If your assessment submission contains fabricated materials, this is likely to affect your marks in each of the assessment criteria, and your assessment may be submitted for integrity review.

·        If AI/MT is used, it must be acknowledged on the cover sheet.

·        To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.

Library Materials and Gen-AI: Students are also reminded that library materials may not be entered into AI technologies without express permission: Policies and guidelines - Library - The University of Queensland

“You may not share, re-publish, copy or enter into AI technologies any part of a resource accessed from our online collections unless expressly permitted in the licence terms for that resource”

“Except as permitted under each Publisher's terms and conditions of use, you must not:

Use UQ Library resource content as an input to generative AI products or services”.

For referencing AI/MT see the Library guide: https://guides.library.uq.edu.au/referencing/ai-tools-assignments

For referencing AI in MLA style see: How do I cite generative AI in MLA style? from the official MLA style website.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission: Fill in the Creative Response template on BB which will include your reflection on process and photos/scans of the zine/creative journal (if chosen) or scene rewrite. Upload a file to the Turnitin link via the course BB site. The link for submission will become available in the folder the day before the due date.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This assessment component will involve undertaking a research project and evidencing the scaffolding of your research process as well as the final product. Students may develop their own research topic on 1 or 2 plays from the course (it can be on the play chosen for the Creative Response). The topic should engage with an aspect of at least one of the 4 themes of the course: power and monarchy; the supernatural; nature and pastoral space; gender and race. The Research Essay will comprise:

·        an abstract (250 words),

·        a scaffolding portfolio (3 handwritten pages – scans/photos to be included in the portfolio), comprising: a character map/s, mind map of the research topic and 1 page draft outline); and

·        the final research essay (1500 words). This should include a Works Cited list that includes any course readings relevant to your topic as well as at least 3 scholarly secondary sources that you have found yourself via the MLA International Bibliography (via the UQ Library).

MLA style should be used for the abstract and final research essay. See the online guide OWL MLA. https://owl.purdue.edu/owl/research_and_citation/mla_style/mla_formatting_and_style_guide/mla_formatting_and_style_guide.html

Criteria:

·        Abstract: clear and coherent outline of the research essay including key data and signposting (5 marks)

·        Scaffolding portfolio: evidencing aspects of the research process and exploring ideas (10 marks)

·        Analysis and Argument, supported by quotations from the play/s (10 marks)

·        Use of scholarly secondary sources (10 marks)

·        Presentation and Writing (10 marks)

Submission: Fill in the Research Essay template on BB which will comprise: a cover sheet; the abstract; scaffolding portfolio (photos/scans of the 3 handwritten pages); and the final essay. Upload a file to the Turnitin link via the course BB site. The link for submission will become available in the folder the day before the due date.

 

AI Policy

Open Assessment: In this assessment you can use resources such as your notes, course materials, and other tools, including AI, while completing the task. The focus is not on memorising information, but on understanding, applying judgement, and demonstrating your own thinking.

Note that:

·        while this assessment is classified as open assessment, so the use of AI is permissible, students should be aware that the use of AI may result in errors and fabricated information and sources eg non-existent quotations or invented secondary materials. If your assessment submission contains fabricated materials, this is likely to affect your marks in each of the assessment criteria, and your assessment may be submitted for integrity review.

·        If AI/MT is used, it must be acknowledged on the cover sheet and referenced in the Works Cited list.

·        To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Fill in the Research Essay template on BB which will comprise: a cover sheet; the abstract; scaffolding portfolio (photos/scans of the 3 handwritten pages); and the final essay. Upload a file to the Turnitin link via the course BB site. The link for submission will become available in the folder the day before the due date.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 10: Research and Writing Techniques

Tutorial 8: Research prep: discussion on scholarly sources and sample abstract and annotated bibliography; demo on using databases. Activity using the MLA International Bibliography database through the UQ library to find 3 scholarly sources you think would be useful for your research

 

Bring to class: your ideas for your research essay; your completed/attempted worksheet and pen and paper for the writing exercise.

Set readings:

Recommended:

·        Sword, Helen. The Writer's Diet. Auckland University Press, 2015. [Available via the UQ library]

https://search.library.uq.edu.au/permalink/61UQ_INST/t5bodr/alma991014429996603131

·        Sword, Helen. Stylish Academic Writing. Harvard University Press, 2012. [Available via the UQ library]

https://search.library.uq.edu.au/discovery/fulldisplay?docid=cdi_unesco_unesdoc_ark_48223_pf0000262776&context=PC&vid=61UQ_INST:61UQ&lang=en&search_scope=61UQ_All&adaptor=Primo%20Central&tab=61UQ_All&query=any,contains,Sword,%20Helen.%20Stylish%20Academic%20Writing&facet=rtype,exclude,newspaper_articles,lk&facet=rtype,exclude,reviews,lk&offset=0

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04

learning_activities.topic:
Week 11: The Tragedy of Mariam (1613)

Lecture 10: The Tragedy of Mariam (1613) - Elizabeth Cary

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 11: The Tragedy of Mariam (1613) Lecture 10: The Tragedy of Mariam (1613) - Elizabeth Cary

learning_activities.topic:
Week 11: The Tragedy of Mariam

Tutorial 9: discussion of The Tragedy of Mariam; Peer review workshop: of Draft Abstracts and Annotated Bibliography; writing exercise.

To Do: Write a Draft Abstract for your research topic (see the sample on BB) and an Annotated Bibliography with 3 entries. Print out 3 hard copies.

Bring to class: your copy of the play; your completed/attempted worksheet; and 3 hard copies of your draft abstract and annotated bibliography for peer review and workshopping.

Set readings:

Required:

·        The Tragedy of Mariam (1613) (estimated reading time 2.5-3 hrs)

Recommended:

o  Introduction to the New Mermaids edition, Cary, Elizabeth. The Tragedy of Mariam (1613), Bloomsbury Publishing, 2010. ed. Karen Britland, pp7-29. The tragedy of Mariam : the fair queen of Jewry / Elizabeth Cary ; edited by Karen Britland - University of Queensland (uq.edu.au) [estimated reading time 1.25 hrs]

o  Rose, Mary Beth ‘The Tragedy of Mariam: political legitimacy and maternal authority’ in The Cambridge Companion to English Renaissance Tragedy, Cambridge UP, 2010, pp.211-221. (available via BB) [estimated reading time 45 mins]

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04

learning_activities.topic:
Week 12 The Tempest (1611)

Tutorial 10: discussion of The Tempest; small group activities; writing exercise.

Bring to class: your copy of the play; your completed/attempted worksheet and pen and paper for the writing exercise.  

Set readings:

Required:

·        The Tempest (estimated reading time 2.5-3 hrs)

·        Garber chapter on The Tempest. Gaber, Marjorie Shakespeare After All. Anchor Books, 2005. Available online: Shakespeare after All - University of Queensland (uq.edu.au) (1 hr)

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04

learning_activities.topic:
Week 13: No Lecture

No lecture. An optional make up tutorial will be held in the lecture slot (and at the lecture venue): If you have missed a tutorial, this is an opportunity to make up the marks for 1 tutorial for the Management of Learning component by attending this alternative session. Students are expected to have completed the preparation for the missed tutorial prior to attending this session.
